title: Inspect And Lubricate Tipper Z-Axis Linear Bearings
summary: Preventive maintenance procedure to inspect both tipper Z-axis linear rails for grease condition and lubricate the bearing blocks when the rails are dry.
when_to_use: Use this procedure during tipper preventive maintenance when checking Z-axis linear rail grease condition and when the rails are dry (not wet with grease).
not_for: Do not use wiping grease on the rails as a substitute for bearing block lubrication.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Inspect the Z-axis linear rails for grease condition and lubricate the bearing blocks if the rails are dry.

safety_notes: This procedure is not marked operator-safe in the source candidate and was assigned to L2_support because it involves maintenance access and component lubrication.
Use a higher-safe role because this is preventive maintenance involving component lubrication and source text does not define operator-safe access conditions.
access_or_tools_needed: Visual access to both Z-axis linear rails
Access to the two bearing blocks and four grease nipples
Automotive bearing grease such as Mobil1™
Means to apply 1.0 to 3.0 cc of grease to each bearing block

access_required: Visual access to both Z-axis linear rails
Access to the two bearing blocks and four grease nipples
system_preconditions: Rails can be visually inspected for wet grease or dried grease condition.

visual_references.description: Tipper component identification reference for locating the tipper assembly and Z-axis area.

steps.title: Inspect both Z-axis linear rails for grease condition
steps.purpose: Determine the current grease condition of the Z-axis linear rails before deciding whether lubrication is needed.
steps.instruction: Visually inspect both Z-axis linear rails for wetness of grease and for signs of dried grease.
steps.expected_result: The grease condition of both Z-axis linear rails is identified.
steps.healthy_condition: Rails appear wet with grease and do not show a dry condition requiring lubrication.
steps.failure_condition: Rails appear dry or show signs of dried grease.

actions.label: Inspect Z-axis linear rails
actions.exact_action: Visually inspect both Z-axis linear rails for wetness of grease and/or signs of dried grease.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the tipper components diagram to orient to the tipper assembly and Z-axis area before inspecting both linear rails.

stop_or_escalate_if: Access conditions needed to inspect the rails are not available or are not defined by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Visually inspect both Z-axis linear rails for wetness of grease and/or signs of dried grease.

steps.title: Determine whether the rails are dry
steps.purpose: Apply the source decision point that determines whether lubrication is required.
steps.instruction: Determine whether the rails are dry, meaning they are not wet with grease.
steps.expected_result: A clear decision is made on whether bearing block lubrication is required.
steps.healthy_condition: Rails are not dry and remain wet with grease.
steps.failure_condition: Rails are dry (not wet with grease).

actions.label: Assess dry condition
actions.exact_action: Determine whether the rails are dry (not wet with grease).

stop_or_escalate_if: The grease condition cannot be confidently determined from visual inspection.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If the rails are dry (not wet with grease) then lubricate the bearing blocks using automotive bearing grease (e.g., Mobil1™).

steps.title: Lubricate the bearing blocks if the rails are dry
steps.purpose: Restore lubrication to the bearing blocks when the inspection shows the rails are dry.
steps.instruction: If the rails are dry, lubricate the bearing blocks using automotive bearing grease such as Mobil1™.
steps.expected_result: Bearing block lubrication is performed using the specified grease type when dry rails are found.
steps.healthy_condition: Specified automotive bearing grease is used to lubricate the bearing blocks when rails are dry.
steps.failure_condition: Dry rails are found but bearing blocks are not lubricated, or an unsupported lubrication method is used.

actions.label: Lubricate bearing blocks
actions.exact_action: If the rails are dry then lubricate the bearing blocks using automotive bearing grease (e.g., Mobil1™).

stop_or_escalate_if: Required grease or application means is not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If the rails are dry (not wet with grease) then lubricate the bearing blocks using automotive bearing grease (e.g., Mobil1™).

steps.title: Locate and grease all four grease nipples on the two bearing blocks
steps.purpose: Ensure lubrication is applied at the documented lubrication points on both bearing blocks.
steps.instruction: Locate the two bearing blocks and grease the standard 1/4-in. nipple on each end of each block, for a total of four nipples.
steps.expected_result: All four documented grease nipples are identified and greased.
steps.healthy_condition: Two bearing blocks are located and grease is applied through one standard 1/4-in. nipple on each end of each block.
steps.failure_condition: Any bearing block or nipple is missed, or grease is not applied through the documented nipples.

actions.label: Grease four nipples
actions.exact_action: There are two (2) bearing blocks with one standard 1/4-in. nipple on each end each, so there are four (4) nipples to grease.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the tipper components diagram to orient to the tipper assembly while locating the bearing block area for lubrication points.

stop_or_escalate_if: The bearing blocks or grease nipples cannot be located from available source-backed references.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: There are two (2) bearing blocks with one standard 1/4-in. nipple on each end each, so there are four (4) nipples to grease.

steps.title: Apply 1.0 to 3.0 cc of grease to each bearing block
steps.purpose: Apply the source-specified grease quantity to each bearing block.
steps.instruction: Add 1.0 to 3.0 cc of grease to each bearing block.
steps.expected_result: Each bearing block receives the documented grease quantity.
steps.healthy_condition: Each bearing block receives 1.0 to 3.0 cc of grease.
steps.failure_condition: Grease quantity per block is outside the documented 1.0 to 3.0 cc range or is not applied.

actions.label: Apply grease quantity
actions.exact_action: Adding 1.0 to 3.0 cc of grease to each block should be adequate.

stop_or_escalate_if: The grease quantity cannot be measured or applied as specified by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Adding 1.0 to 3.0 cc of grease to each block should be adequate.

steps.title: Do not use grease wiped on the rails as the lubrication method
steps.purpose: Prevent use of an explicitly inadequate lubrication method.
steps.instruction: Do not treat wiping grease on the rails as adequate lubrication.
steps.expected_result: Lubrication is performed through the bearing blocks rather than by wiping grease on the rails.
steps.healthy_condition: Bearing block lubrication is used and grease is not merely wiped on the rails.
steps.failure_condition: Grease is only wiped on the rails instead of lubricating the bearing blocks.

actions.label: Verify lubrication method
actions.exact_action: NOTE: Wiping grease on the rails is not adequate.

stop_or_escalate_if: The only available lubrication approach is wiping grease on the rails.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: NOTE: Wiping grease on the rails is not adequate.

success_criteria: Both Z-axis linear rails have been inspected for grease condition.
If the rails were dry, both bearing blocks were lubricated using automotive bearing grease such as Mobil1™.
All four standard 1/4-in. grease nipples were greased.
Each bearing block received 1.0 to 3.0 cc of grease.
Lubrication was not performed by merely wiping grease on the rails.
healthy_conditions: Z-axis linear rails are wet with grease or, if dry, the bearing blocks have been lubricated per the documented method.
Two bearing blocks and four grease nipples are serviced as described by the source.
Grease quantity applied is 1.0 to 3.0 cc per bearing block.
failure_conditions: Rails are dry and bearing blocks are not lubricated.
Any of the four grease nipples is not greased.
Grease quantity per bearing block is not within the documented 1.0 to 3.0 cc range.
Grease is wiped on the rails instead of lubricating the bearing blocks.
Required access, grease, or application means is unavailable.
escalation_guidance: Escalate to appropriate maintenance support if safe access conditions for inspection or lubrication are not defined or available.
Escalate if the bearing blocks or grease nipples cannot be located using the available source-backed references.
Escalate if the required grease type or a means to apply 1.0 to 3.0 cc per block is unavailable.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Tipper assembly orientation and Z-axis area reference for locating the inspection and lubrication area.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Visually inspect both Z-axis linear rails for wetness of grease and/or signs of dried grease.
If the rails are dry (not wet with grease) then lubricate the bearing blocks using automotive bearing grease (e.g., Mobil1™).
There are two (2) bearing blocks with one standard 1/4-in. nipple on each end each, so there are four (4) nipples to grease.
Adding 1.0 to 3.0 cc of grease to each block should be adequate.
NOTE: Wiping grease on the rails is not adequate.
